title: Probability and Statistics
description: Introduction to probability theory and statistical methods with applications in computer science. Covers sample spaces, conditional probability, Bayes theorem, random variables, common distributions (binomial, Poisson, normal, exponential), expectation and variance, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, and linear regression. Applications include algorithm analysis, machine learning foundations, network reliability, and data analysis. This course provides the statistical foundation needed for machine learning and data science coursework. Students learn to use statistical software (R or Python) for data analysis projects.
outcomes: Apply probability rules to compute event probabilities
Model real-world phenomena using probability distributions
Perform hypothesis tests and construct confidence intervals
Apply Bayes theorem to update beliefs with evidence
Use statistical software for data analysis
